prompt:
A NexusOrder Desk platform capable of running a complete, offline commerce and fulfillment workflow for an internship-program office and its approved vendors, with enterprise-grade controls for multi-role operations. The application targets Windows 11 and is delivered as a signed .msi installer; the UI language is English and must render correctly at 1920x1080 and above with High DPI scaling. Users authenticate locally with username + password (minimum 12 characters, at least one number and one symbol, lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts). Roles include Student (places requests), Faculty Advisor (approves/denies), Corporate Mentor (confirms receipt/condition), Department Admin (catalog, rules, finance, audits). Desktop interaction is keyboard-first: Ctrl+K opens global search, Ctrl+Enter submits checkout, and Alt+1/2 switches between Orders and Rules; right-click context menus support "Split Order," "Merge Orders," "Create RMA," and "Tag/Note." Multi-window operation is required so admins can keep an Order Detail window open while reconciling payments in a separate window. A system tray mode keeps background automation running (timeouts, reminders) while minimizing disruption, with configurable on-screen notifications at milestones (order placed, approved, shipped, delivered, refund issued) stored locally rather than relying on push services.

All logic runs locally using Electron for the desktop shell, React for UI, Express for the embedded local service layer, and MongoDB as the on-device database with automatic daily encrypted backups to a user-selected folder plus one-click restore. End-to-end order management includes cart/checkout, an explicit order state machine (Draft -> Submitted -> Approved -> Paid -> Allocated -> Shipped -> Delivered -> Closed, with After-Sales sub-states), configurable auto-cancel of unpaid orders after 30 minutes, and auto-close 14 days after delivery unless an RMA is opened. Order split/merge must preserve invoice fields, sales tax line items, notes/tags, and immutable audit history; after-sales supports return/refund/exchange with standardized reason codes editable by admins. "WeChat Pay" is implemented as an offline reconciliation module: staff import a locally provided statement file (CSV) and the system verifies row signatures using a pre-provisioned merchant public key, enforces idempotency via unique payment intent IDs, flags duplicates, and supports payment exception repair (e.g., mark "Paid-Unreconciled" with mandatory admin note). Risk controls include per-user submission throttles (max 10 checkout attempts per 10 minutes), optional local device fingerprinting (hashed hardware + OS attributes stored with consent), and a blacklist that blocks checkout but allows browsing. Data protection requires TLS for localhost IPC, AES-256 at-rest encryption for sensitive fields, masking (show last 4 digits/characters) in grids, RBAC down to menu and API routes, and data-scope filtering by school/major/class/cohort. The rules engine provides a visual editor for scope, priority, conditions, and time windows, plus simulation against historical orders, conflict and circular-dependency detection, and auto-remediation suggestions (e.g., "lower priority" or "add mutually exclusive condition"). The app must stay stable in week-long sessions with explicit resource cleanup to prevent Electron memory leaks, automatic state recovery after crashes using write-ahead checkpoints, and offline update via imported package with version rollback to the previous build if startup health checks fail.

Frontend stack

modern_frontend_framework

Backend stack

Node.js

Database

MongoDB.
